text:
BOTTOM LINE — Risk-off, cyclicals dumped, defensives bid, commodities whipsawed.  
Tech-led weakness in QQQ -0.62% and small-cap underperformance in IWM -1.08% signaled profit-taking in recent winners and renewed caution around growth sensitivity. Hawkish macro prints with CPI YoY 3.95% and PCE YoY 3.50% kept rates anxiety elevated, feeding into pressure on duration plays like TLT -0.65% and growth sectors. Energy strength with XLE +1.17% and USO +2.46% plus metals capitulation with SLV -4.35% and GDX -3.86% showed a sharp rotation out of crowded reflation/metals trades into defensives like XLV +1.10% and XLU +0.91%.

INDICES — VIX 18.1 rose vs 20d MA 17.9, indicating modestly higher equity vol. Returns ranked: RSP -0.58%, DIA -0.61%, QQQ -0.62%, SPY -0.67%, IJR -0.92%, IJH -0.97%, IWM -1.08%, showing broad but small-cap-heavy selling. STYLE line showed LARGE > SMALL by 0.30pp with large -0.62% vs small -0.92%, confirming size pressure. Most notable cross-asset move was SLV -4.35% vs GLD -1.66%, signaling aggressive precious-metals de-risking rather than classic risk-off bid to gold. SPY above 200-day MA (level implied by ytd +7.40%) and QQQ above 200-day MA (ytd +14.42%) kept CTA trend signals intact.

VOLUME — SPY volume was implied near average with index complex avg 1.05x while QQQ ran at 1.1x, flagging more active trading in tech weakness. Elevated volume in XLK 1.4x with price -0.64% and XLB 1.1x with price -2.35% pointed to distribution in tech and materials, while XLV 1.3x with price +1.10% showed active accumulation in healthcare. Depressed volume in XLE 0.8x and XLRE 0.8x with price gains in XLE +1.17% and XLRE +0.43% suggested low-conviction chasing in cyclicals and rate-sensitives.

SECTORS — XLE Energy +1.17%, XLV Healthcare +1.10%, XLU Utilities +0.91%, XLRE Real Estate +0.43%, XLP Staples +0.22%, XLK Technology -0.64%, XLC Comms -0.97%, XLY Discretionary -1.11%, XLI Industrials -1.18%, XLF Financials -1.24%, XLB Materials -2.35%. Top industries were UNG Natural Gas +3.12% and USO Oil +2.46% while bottom industries were MSOS Cannabis -4.56% and SILJ Junior Silver Miners -4.77%.

THE 7s — Top baskets: Medtech +1.33%, Health +1.27%, Energy +0.52%, Power +0.45%, Old Staples +0.17%. Bottom baskets: New Metals -2.86%, Old Metals -2.91%, Bank -1.63%, Buildout -1.95%, Housing -1.42%. Strongest single names likely sat in Energy (e.g., integrated majors) and Medtech (e.g., device leaders), while weakest names were in metals baskets (e.g., steel and silver miners) and Bank basket (e.g., regional lenders), with metals showing clear internal correlation rather than divergence.

FED & LIQUIDITY — Next FOMC is Jun 17, 2026 with CME probabilities at hold 100%, cut 0%, hike 0% and stance tally Hawk 6, Neutral 5, Dove 4, consistent with the hawkish macro mix (CPI YoY 3.3%, unemployment 4.3%). USD liquidity stress at 32/100 flagged normal conditions, with Reserve Scarcity 3,102,810B at the 79th percentile as the most-stressed component. TED spread at 0.09% and ON-RRP at $12.9B signaled no acute funding stress despite elevated MOVE 85.3.

POSITIONING — SPY PCR 1.04 and QQQ PCR 1.35 with positive skews of 9.2% and 9.1% showed meaningful downside protection demand and put premium. IWM PCR 3.48 with skew 5.5% revealed intense small-cap hedging pressure, matching IWM -1.08% price action. VIX 18.1 and VXN 24.1 both slightly above 20d MAs plus MOVE 85.3 above its 20d 72.8 highlighted cross-asset vol stress centered in rates and growth equity.

CROSS-ASSET — Dollar strength with UUP +0.32% plus higher yields (TLT -0.65%, IEF -0.39%) pressured metals with GLD -1.66% and SLV -4.35%, signaling real-rate worries rather than pure growth fears. Oil and gas outperformance with USO +2.46% and UNG +3.12% pulled XLE +1.17% and XOP +1.33% higher even as cyclicals like XLI -1.18% and XLF -1.24% slumped, suggesting sector-specific energy positioning rather than broad macro optimism.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed at 55.2/100 sat neutral, confirming balanced but cautious risk appetite. Top movers across the tape included MCHI +2.71% (Countries), VRT (data centers, intraday +17.1% off lows), and MSOS -4.56% (Cannabis), while worst were SILJ -4.77% (Metals), SLV -4.35% (Metals), and GDX -3.86% (Metals). Best 20d momentum sector was XLK +11.99% and worst was XLB -5.27%, while best basket momentum was Semi +30.83% and worst was Housing -12.75%. Vol spikes in UTES 13.4x with price +0.39% indicated utilities accumulation, while ROBO 3.9x with price -2.05% and SLX 2.5x with price -2.25% flagged distribution in robotics and steel after recent growth and metals runs.

WATCH — SLV -4.35% with ATM_IV 57.7%, skew +9.5%, GEX_flip 58.0, and PCR 0.29 deserves focus for potential short-covering or further downside if dealers remain short gamma. IWM -1.08% with PCR 3.48 and SIZE factor still in BULL regime (zscore +0.27, days 497) is key for any capitulation or reversal in small-caps versus large. XLK -0.64% with 1.4x volume and 20d momentum +11.99% sits at the intersection of crowded AI/growth positioning and rising rates via MOVE 85.3, making it critical for near-term index direction.
